findings.description: The NC Water Resources Environmental Quality logo appears slightly different in styling between the PDF and HTML versions, but is present and correctly placed in both.
findings.evidence: Logo is present in both versions at the top of the document. Minor visual differences in rendering are acceptable.

findings.description: The PDF has a right-aligned header block with Governor name, Secretary name, and Director name positioned in the upper right corner, while the HTML renders these as left-aligned block elements below the logo, collapsing the two-column header layout.
findings.evidence: PDF: PAT MCCRORY (Governor), DONALD R. VAN DER VAART (Secretary), S. JAY ZIMMERMAN (Director) are right-aligned. HTML: Same names are left-aligned below the logo in a single column.

findings.description: The 'MEMORANDUM' title loses its underline formatting in the HTML version.
findings.evidence: PDF: 'MEMORANDUM' is underlined and centered. HTML: 'MEMORANDUM' is rendered as a heading without underline, with a horizontal rule below it instead.

findings.description: The memo header fields (To, From, Subject) lose their tabular/indented alignment in the HTML, but the information is preserved.
findings.evidence: PDF uses consistent indentation with labels left-aligned and values indented. HTML renders them as flowing text blocks.

findings.description: Footer information (State of North Carolina address and phone number) appears between the memo content and the History/Background section in the HTML, disrupting reading flow.
findings.evidence: PDF: Footer at bottom of page 1. HTML: Footer text appears inline between the outline list and the History/Background section.

findings.description: In the oral comments summary, topic headings lose their underline formatting in the HTML version.
findings.evidence: PDF: Topic headings like 'Air Quality', 'Beneficial Reuse', 'Costs' etc. are bold and underlined. HTML: Same headings are bold but not underlined.

findings.description: In the written public comments summary, topic headings lose their underline formatting in the HTML version.
findings.evidence: PDF: Topic headings like 'Air Quality', 'Groundwater Assessment', etc. are bold and underlined. HTML: Same headings are bold but not underlined.

findings.description: S. JAY ZIMMERMAN rendered as an ordered list item instead of plain text
findings.evidence: The PDF shows 'S. JAY ZIMMERMAN / Director' as a header block element. The HTML wraps it in '<ol type="A"><li>JAY ZIMMERMAN...' which renders it as 'A. JAY ZIMMERMAN' instead of 'S. JAY ZIMMERMAN'.

findings.description: Page footer/header content repeated inline between sections
findings.evidence: After the outline list and before Section I, the HTML includes the footer text 'State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality | Water Resources / 1611 Mail service Center | Raleigh, North Carolina 27699-1611 / 919 707 9000' plus a bottom banner image. This is a page footer artifact from page 1 that appears inline in the content flow.

findings.description: S. JAY ZIMMERMAN's initial 'S.' is lost due to ordered list formatting
findings.evidence: PDF: 'S. JAY ZIMMERMAN'. HTML: '<ol type="A"><li>JAY ZIMMERMAN' which renders as 'A. JAY ZIMMERMAN'. The 'S.' is replaced by the list marker 'A.', losing the correct initial.
